message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 1990","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10884","justification":"National survey of the German population covering political attitudes, political participation, party preferences, trust in institutions, and socio‑economic evaluations in 1990, directly relevant to political behaviour and economic views in Germany in the early 1990s."},{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 1992","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10885","justification":"Covers political attitudes, political interest, party identification, satisfaction with democracy, assessments of government performance, and economic evaluations for Germany in 1992, matching the topics of political behaviour and economic systems."},{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 1994","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10886","justification":"Includes questions on political efficacy, party attachment, voting behaviour, government institutions, and economic situation assessments in Germany in 1994, fitting the requested topic areas in the mid‑1990s."},{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 1996","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10887","justification":"National survey with modules on political participation, trust in political institutions, satisfaction with democracy, attitudes towards market and welfare policies, and socio‑economic development perceptions in Germany in 1996."},{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 1998","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10888","justification":"End‑of‑decade ALLBUS wave containing detailed measures of political behaviour and attitudes, party preferences, evaluations of the political system, and perceptions of economic conditions in Germany in 1998."},{"title":"Political Attitudes, Political Participation and Voter Behaviour in United Germany 1990","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4140","justification":"Focuses on political attitudes, trust in institutions, democratic orientations, and voting behaviour in unified Germany shortly after reunification, directly addressing political behaviour and government system perceptions in 1990."},{"title":"German Federal Election Study 1990 (Wahlstudie 1990)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4141","justification":"Election study concentrating on voter behaviour, party choice, campaign evaluation, and attitudes towards the political system in the 1990 Bundestag election, matching the elections and political behaviour dimension for Germany in the early 1990s."},{"title":"German Federal Election Study 1994 (Wahlstudie 1994)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4143","justification":"Provides detailed information on electoral participation, vote choice, political attitudes, and views on government performance during the 1994 Bundestag election in Germany, linking elections, political behaviour and government evaluations."},{"title":"German Federal Election Study 1998 (Wahlstudie 1998)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4145","justification":"Covers the 1998 Bundestag election with rich data on turnout, party preference, evaluations of parties and government, and attitudes towards economic and social policy, relevant to elections, political systems and economic development in late‑1990s Germany."},{"title":"German Socio-Economic Panel (SOEP) – Cross-National Equivalent File, Germany 1990s extracts (via GESIS)","url_or_doi":"https://dbk.gesis.org/dbksearch/sdesc2.asp?no=2730&db=e","justification":"Longitudinal micro-data on households and individuals in Germany including political interest, satisfaction with democracy and government, and extensive measures of economic conditions and development throughout the 1990s; accessible via GESIS with multiple yearly waves in that decade."}]}
